title: Memory System
description: The Memory interface persists deployment state to disk, a database, or any backend.
overview_body: Every render() call requires a Memory implementation. The runtime calls getState at startup to restore previous state and saveState after each render cycle to persist changes. CReact does not provide a built-in implementation. You bring your own.
method_get_state_desc: Retrieve previously saved state. Return null on first run.
method_save_state_desc: Persist the current state after each render cycle.
method_acquire_lock_desc: Acquire a deploy lock to prevent concurrent deploys. Returns true if acquired.
method_append_audit_log_desc: Append an entry to the deployment audit trail.
method_get_audit_log_desc: Retrieve audit log entries for a stack.
deployment_state_body: The state object contains the full snapshot of all nodes, their props, outputs, and deployment status.
lifecycle_step_render: render() is called with your Memory implementation
lifecycle_step_get_state: Runtime calls memory.getState(stackName) to load previous state
lifecycle_step_hydrate_outputs: Saved output values are hydrated into nodes (so child components can read them immediately)
lifecycle_step_diff: The reconciler diffs new JSX tree against saved nodes
lifecycle_step_rerun: Handlers re-run for all nodes to re-establish side effects. They receive previously saved outputs and should be idempotent
lifecycle_step_save_state: After the cycle, memory.saveState(stackName, state) persists the result
crash_recovery_body: If the process crashes mid-deploy, applyingNodeId records which node was in progress. On restart, the runtime resumes from that node instead of starting over.
enables_crash_recovery: Crash recovery: nodes with saved outputs are treated as deployed
enables_incremental_deploys: Incremental deploys: only changed resources are re-applied
enables_drift_detection: Drift detection: compare expected state vs actual
enables_concurrency_protection: Concurrency protection: optional locking prevents parallel deploys
enables_audit_trail: Audit trail: optional logging tracks deployment history
backend_options_intro: The interface works with any storage backend. Common implementations:
backend_dynamodb_desc: Serverless, low-latency, supports conditional writes for locking
backend_redis_desc: Fast, TTL-based cleanup for short-lived stacks
tip_minimal_example: For a minimal working example, see Getting Started: State and Memory.
